   "source": [
    "# Non-Gaussian Noise Analysis: Rayleigh and Gaussian-Chi\n",
    "\n",
    "This tutorial demonstrates the implementation of a comprehensive non-Gaussian noise analysis toolkit in GWexpy, based on research by Yamamura (2024) and Yamamoto (2015-2016).\n",
    "\n",
    "### Contents\n",
    "1. **Noise Simulation**: Generating non-stationary and scattered light noise.\n",
    "2. **Rayleigh Statistics**: Testing deviations from Rayleigh distribution.\n",
    "3. **GauCh (Modified KS Test)**: Advanced non-Gaussianity detection.\n",
    "4. **Student-t Indicator**: Measuring distribution tail thickness.\n",
    "5. **Data Quality Flags**: Generating veto segments automatically.\n",
    "6. **Evaluation & Visualization**: ROC curves and composite dashboards."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "fff52b96",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:33:16.834630Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:33:16.834400Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.245857Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.245305Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 6.424547,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.247667+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:33:16.823120+00:00",
     "status": "completed"

   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.noise import scatter_light_noise, transient_gaussian_noise\n",
    "from gwexpy.plot.gauch_dashboard import plot_gauch_dashboard\n",
    "from gwexpy.statistics import to_segments\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5c5c9f46",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.008595,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.266090+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.257495+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Noise Simulation\n",
    "We simulate two types of non-Gaussian noise models used in KAGRA characterization.\n",
    "- **Model I**: Transient Gaussian noise (glitches).\n",
    "- **Model II**: Scattered light noise (stationary non-Gaussianity)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "210d84a8",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.315310Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.314811Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.831234Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.829822Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 37.538708,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.836500+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:33:23.297792+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 1200x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 3,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 1200x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "duration = 32.0\n",
    "fs = 1024.0\n",
    "\n",
    "# Model I: Glitch injection\n",
    "ts_glitch = transient_gaussian_noise(duration, fs, A1=20.0, name='Glitchy Data')\n",
    "\n",
    "# Model II: Scattered light\n",
    "ts_scatter = scatter_light_noise(duration, fs, A2=1e-12, name='Scattered Light')\n",
    "\n",
    "ts_glitch.plot()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b1ef161a",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.012396,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.861477+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.849081+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Rayleigh Spectrogram\n",
    "The Rayleigh statistic $R$ measures the consistency of the ASD distribution with a Rayleigh distribution. We use the `rayleigh_test` method to get a p-value map."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "72142fa5",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.904629Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.904335Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.468273Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.463368Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 2.57877,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.470574+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:00.891804+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rs_p = ts_glitch.rayleigh_test(fftlength=0.4, stride=0.5, overlap=0.2, n_samples=20)\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))\n",
    "mesh = ax.pcolormesh(rs_p.times.value, rs_p.frequencies.value, rs_p.value.T, shading='auto')\n",
    "ax.set_yscale('log')\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel('Time (s)')\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('Frequency (Hz)')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Rayleigh Test p-value Map')\n",
    "plt.colorbar(mappable=mesh, ax=ax, label='p-value')\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8ca95797",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.013056,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.495979+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.482923+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. GauCh (Modified KS Test)\n",
    "GauCh is a more sensitive test for non-Gaussianity using a modified Kolmogorov-Smirnov test."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "c9546d8f",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.549525Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.549320Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.562265Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.556207Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 1.031534,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.565854+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:03.534320+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "gauch_res = ts_glitch.gauch(fftlength=1.0, window=8)\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))\n",
    "mesh = ax.pcolormesh(gauch_res.pvalue_map.times.value, gauch_res.pvalue_map.frequencies.value, gauch_res.pvalue_map.value.T, shading='auto')\n",
    "ax.set_yscale('log')\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel('Time (s)')\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('Frequency (Hz)')\n",
    "ax.set_title('GauCh p-value Map')\n",
    "plt.colorbar(mappable=mesh, ax=ax, label='p-value')\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "76781817",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.020852,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.610974+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.590122+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 4. Student-t Indicator\n",
    "The Student-t indicator fits a Student-t distribution to FFT components and outputs the degree of freedom $\\nu$."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "22f707a4",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.707576Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.707134Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:41:55.087561Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:41:55.086878Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 470.418109,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:41:55.100971+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:34:04.682862+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "nu_spec = ts_glitch.student_t_spectrogram(fftlength=1.0, window=8, frange=(10, 200))\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))\n",
    "mesh = ax.pcolormesh(nu_spec.times.value, nu_spec.frequencies.value, nu_spec.value.T, shading='auto')\n",
    "ax.set_yscale('log')\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel('Time (s)')\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('Frequency (Hz)')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Student-t nu Map')\n",
    "plt.colorbar(mappable=mesh, ax=ax, label='nu')\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {

   },
   "source": [
    "## 5. Data Quality Flags\n",
    "We can automatically generate veto segments where the p-value is below a threshold."
   ]
  },

   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "62e17d65",

   "source": [
    "dq_flag = to_segments(gauch_res.pvalue_map, alpha=0.001)\n",
    "print(dq_flag)\n",
    "dq_flag.plot()"
   ]
  },
